claimText: The BBNJ Agreement (High Seas Treaty) is a legally binding treaty under UNCLOS aimed at conserving marine biodiversity beyond national jurisdiction.
evidenceExcerpt: Covers the BBNJ Agreement (High Seas Treaty), a legally binding treaty under UNCLOS aimed at conserving marine biodiversity beyond national jurisdiction.
examUse: Statement-based question on the legal nature and primary objective of the BBNJ Agreement.
distractorHooks: It is a voluntary, non-binding guideline
It is an agreement under the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD)
It applies to biodiversity within a nation's Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ)

claimText: The key pillars of the BBNJ Agreement include the establishment of Marine Protected Areas (MPAs), management of Marine Genetic Resources (MGRs), and conducting Environmental Impact Assessments.
evidenceExcerpt: It outlines key pillars like Marine Protected Areas (MPAs), Marine Genetic Resources (MGRs), and Environmental Impact Assessments.
examUse: Multiple-choice question identifying the core components and mechanisms of the BBNJ treaty.

claimText: The BBNJ Agreement is the third implementation agreement under UNCLOS, following the 1994 Part XI and 1995 Fish Stocks agreements.
evidenceExcerpt: BBNJ is the third implementation agreement under UNCLOS (others: 1994 Part XI and 1995 Fish Stocks).
examUse: Statement-based question on the historical context and structure of UNCLOS agreements.
distractorHooks: It is the first implementation agreement under UNCLOS
It is an independent treaty completely separate from UNCLOS

claimText: The BBNJ Agreement requires 60 ratifications to enter into force, and India has signed but not yet ratified it.
evidenceExcerpt: BBNJ requires 60 ratifications to enter into force; India has signed but not ratified.
examUse: Statement-based question on the enforcement conditions of the treaty and India's current participation status.
distractorHooks: India has signed and ratified the agreement
It requires unanimous ratification by all UN members to enter into force
India has neither signed nor ratified the agreement

claimText: Non-UNCLOS members are permitted to join the BBNJ agreement.
evidenceExcerpt: Non-UNCLOS members can also join the BBNJ agreement.
examUse: Statement-based question on the eligibility criteria for joining the High Seas Treaty.
distractorHooks: Only existing UNCLOS members can join the BBNJ agreement
Membership is restricted to coastal states only

claimText: Under UNCLOS, the Territorial Sea extends up to 12 nautical miles with full sovereignty, the Contiguous Zone up to 24 nautical miles, and the Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) up to 200 nautical miles.
evidenceExcerpt: UNCLOS Maritime Zones: Territorial Sea (up to 12 nm, full sovereignty), Contiguous Zone (up to 24 nm), EEZ (up to 200 nm).
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question matching maritime zones to their respective distance limits and sovereignty levels.
distractorHooks: The Contiguous Zone grants full sovereignty up to 24 nm
The EEZ extends up to 12 nm
The Territorial Sea extends up to 200 nm

claimText: The Deep Seabed beyond national jurisdiction is designated as the 'common heritage of mankind' and is managed by the International Seabed Authority (ISA).
evidenceExcerpt: Deep Seabed beyond national jurisdiction is the 'common heritage of mankind', managed by the International Seabed Authority (ISA).
examUse: Institution-feature question on the role of the ISA and the legal status of the deep seabed.
distractorHooks: The deep seabed is managed by the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)
The deep seabed is considered 'terra nullius' and belongs to the first nation to exploit it
It is managed by the UN Security Council
